g.text: B. Coverage A — Dwelling and Coverage B — Other Structures
We insure against risk of direct physical loss to property described in
Coverages A and B.
3. Covered Perils — Weather Events
We cover direct physical loss caused by the following named perils:
a. Windstorm or hail, including ice, snow, or sleet accumulation that
results in structural damage to the roof or exterior walls.
b. Weight of ice, snow, or sleet which causes damage to property
contained in a building.
c. Ice dams — accidental discharge or overflow of water resulting from
the formation of an ice dam on the roof, where interior damage
results from water backup into the dwelling.
d. Sudden and accidental tearing apart, cracking, burning, or bulging
of a steam, hot water, air conditioning, or fire protective system.
C. We do not insure for loss caused by any of the following:
1. Ordinance or Law
Meaning any ordinance or law regulating the construction, repair, or
demolition of a building or other structure.
2. Water Damage — Interior Without Exterior Breach
We do not insure for loss caused by water damage to the interior of
the dwelling unless:
a. The damage is the direct result of a covered peril listed in
Section I — Perils Insured Against, paragraph B.3; or
b. There is evidence of an exterior breach (damaged roof, shingles,
flashing, or wall) that permitted water to enter the dwelling.
Interior water staining absent an exterior breach is excluded under
this provision. This exclusion is modified by the Water Damage
Exclusion Endorsement (HO-04 11) where attached.
3. Earth Movement
Meaning earthquake, landslide, mine subsidence, mudflow, sinkhole,
or any other earth movement.
4. Neglect
Meaning neglect of the "insured" to use all reasonable means to save
and preserve property at and after the time of a loss.
